fields.description:
## Why
rebar's P6 flags a MISSING verify command; it does not catch a PRESENT command that silently lies. A mechanically-checkable subset fits the DET floor (polyglot, fail-open, coverage-recording).

## What (with review-surfaced constraint)
- CONSTRAINT: DET_CHECKS in det_floor.py is FROZEN P1-P9 (ADR 0016) and tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py hard-asserts it. So do NOT add a P10. EXTEND p6_ac_quality (det_floor.py def@493) with the verify-command lint, or justify a floor change against ADR 0016 explicitly.
- Flag: pure file-inspection with no assertion (grep/find/ls/wc/cat/head with no test/comparison), unanchored-grep patterns, unquoted shell metacharacters. Consider shellcheck as prior art before a bespoke regex set.
- Language-agnostic + fail-open: a line in an unparseable stack ABSTAINS (coverage), never false-accuses.
- LLM-tier depth of this taxonomy is WS5/E6 (sibling), not part of this DET story -- keep out of this ticket's ACs.

## Provenance
Labels like G-8 / FP-3a / R-6 / WS-N are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020) -- they are NOT codebase symbols and cannot be grepped. Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] p6 extended (or ADR-0016-reconciled) with the mechanically-checkable verify-command lint. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py` green after update.
- [ ] Fires on each of the three defect classes; abstains on unknown/unparseable lines (recorded as coverage). Proof: fixture set with one bad + one unknown command; unit test asserts fire vs abstain.
- [ ] Prior-art note on shellcheck recorded (adopt or justify bespoke).
